title: Incentive-Fee Management Contract Not A Share Of Net Profits
content: On the facts represented, an incentive-fee management contract between a §501(c)(3) bond issuer and a hotel manager — where the incentive fee is calculated on gross revenue subject to an adjustment contingent on a net-profit-variant metric — does not constitute sharing of net profits under Rev. Proc. 2017-13 §5.02(2) and therefore does not cause private business use under IRC §141. The ruling applies only to the requesting taxpayer on the specific facts presented.
